decision: explicitly supersedes them.

title: Govern stage2_variable_sweep_and_table_compaction_v1 as the maintained S2-2 class-level repair
decision: Treat variable-sweep rescue, evidence-table compaction, duplicate suppression, and S2-2/S2-3 boundary validation as the governed maintained S2-2 repair set.
reason: The validated multi-paper repair set closes missing_variable_table, table_flattening, false_role_coverage, evidence_budget_overflow, and near_duplicate_evidence without sentinel regression.
impact: The maintained Stage2 composite path now records the class-level repair in the repair index, mem_v1, and maintained-script registry for future baseline runs.

title: Decision: Lock the summary-only coverage-first S2-2 / S2-4a selector contract
decision: Keep all S2-4a table evidence summary-only, reduce selector authority to conservative denoising plus minimum coverage plus bounded packing, classify tables only as must_include/optional_context/hard_drop, and return semantic table scoping authority to the LLM.
reason: Targeted replay improved or stabilized INMUTV7L, V99GKZEI, L3H2RS2H, and QLYKLPKT without reintroducing full-table prompt surfaces or coarse-label semantic veto.
impact: Governed Stage2 docs, audit checklist, repair index, and maintained script surfaces must treat selector semantics as coverage-first and summary-only while keeping semantic table judgment LLM-owned.

title: S2-2b confirmed-noise-only table preservation
decision: Irreversible S2-2b table removal is limited to confirmed pure noise; if a table is not confirmed noise, preserve it in the pre-LLM authority surface.
reason: 5ZXYABSU showed that formulation-bearing Table 1 and Table 2 survived S2-2a but were wrongly hard-dropped at S2-2b, creating irreversible summary-only loss before the LLM.
impact: Future selector work must reject importance-based table suppression and treat audit/debug labels as observability rather than preservation vetoes.

title: S2-3/S2-4a summary neutrality and structure-first summary contract
decision: The maintained S2-3/S2-4a summary path is neutral across preserved tables; the main residual risk is lossy compression, and header schema plus first-column row identity are the primary summary contract while sample rows are optional aids only.
reason: Prompt-path audits showed no normal-path primary-table promotion across preserved tables, but structural compression can still hide formulation detail.
impact: Future summary work should preserve schema and row-identity structure rather than reintroducing table-importance ranking or sample-row dependence.

title: Authority reopen metadata is deterministic execution-side metadata
decision: authority_run_dir, authority_payload_root, and related reopen handles are deterministic execution-side metadata and must not be treated as LLM semantic content or depend on the LLM to transmit them.
reason: Replay from frozen raw responses stayed semantically reusable but failed when authority metadata was missing from replayed semantic docs, proving the need for deterministic sidecar/reattachment handling.
impact: Replay and downstream deterministic execution must recover authority handles through governed sidecars or reattachment surfaces rather than semantic-doc dependence.

title: Prefer replay from frozen raw responses when the LLM-facing contract is unchanged
decision: When LLM task, prompt content, evidence content, model, and generation settings are unchanged, downstream deterministic contract changes should prefer replay from frozen raw responses over fresh live calls if execution-side metadata can be lawfully reattached.
reason: Recent Stage2 repairs were entirely downstream of frozen S2-4b raw responses, so replay reduced nondeterminism and kept validation scoped to the changed contract.
impact: Future deterministic Stage2 repairs should default to replay-first validation and use fresh live calls only when the LLM-facing contract changes or replay cannot lawfully reattach metadata.

title: Bounded simple formulation-table deterministic enumeration after semantic authorization
decision: Stage2 may deterministically enumerate base rows for low-ambiguity non-DOE full_formulation tables after LLM table authorization using preserved normalized_table_payload authority; this path does not require LLM row-level output and does not apply to DOE, sweep recovery, or cross-table decode cases.
reason: INMUTV7L preserved a simple numbered formulation table upstream, but the frozen raw response kept only a family-level semantic object. Bounded S2-7 replay validation recovered 12 deterministic rows from preserved authority without changing prompt text or requiring new live calls.
impact: Simple formulation tables can now be recovered deterministically after semantic authorization while WIVUCMYG remains on DOE, 5GIF3D8W remains on non-DOE single-variable recovery, and UFXX9WXE remains stable without regression.

title: Decision: Restore UFXX9WXE DOE target binding with file-derived table IDs and tolerant numbered-row detection
decision: Bounded replay validation restored UFXX9WXE deterministic DOE enumeration to 26 rows by preferring file-derived __table_N__ identity over conflicting caption-derived numbers and by tolerating spaced numbered labels like 7 . during numbered-row detection.
reason: Current live baseline recognized DOE scope but failed to bind Table 10 back to preserved authority because S2-2 recovered the authority asset under the wrong table ID and lost numbered-row structure on the companion table.
impact: UFXX9WXE DOE execution now resumes on governed replay; WIVUCMYG and YGA8VQKU remain stable in the same bounded validation.

title: Decision: Recover corrupted split-column variable-sweep tables with source-backed row recovery
decision: Use bounded source-backed row recovery in table_row_expansion_v1 for LLM-authorized variable-sweep tables whose preserved payload stays repair_insufficient/unrepaired_corrupted.
reason: L3H2RS2H retained lawful rowwise concentration evidence in preserved source CSV and source-text table blocks even though standard direct-row extraction emitted zero rows.
impact: Bounded and full replay restored L3H2RS2H from 7 to 21 final rows without collateral count drift on other papers.

title: Decision: Collapse later measurement-only rows that reuse DOE labels onto existing DOE formulation cores
decision: For DOE papers, later non-DOE measurement/post-processing rows that reuse a unique deterministic DOE formulation label and only add before/after processing characterization variables must collapse onto the existing DOE formulation core rather than create a new formulation.
impact: Stage5 duplicate-governance now collapses these later labeled measurement rows; WIVUCMYG moved from 29/26 to 26/26 and no other paper counts changed in the full compare collateral run.

title: Decision: Recover anchorless sequential-optimization single-variable rows from stagewise source-text contracts when row-level raw replay collapsed to family summaries
decision: When a replayed sequential-optimization paper preserves stagewise optimization semantics and selected-condition hints but no longer materializes row-level candidates, Stage2 may recover synthesis-defining single-variable rows directly from explicit stagewise source-text level lists, provided downstream/post-processing variable axes are excluded and an explicit anchor scope is not already available elsewhere in the document.
impact: Bounded replay restored QLYKLPKT to 7 final rows and full replay improved the compare surface from 12/15 to 13/15 matches with no collateral count drift after guardrailing against papers with existing explicit anchor scopes.

title: 5ZXYABSU shared-parameter evidence from user paper check
decision: For 5ZXYABSU, PVA is the stabilizer; Polysorbate 80 and Labrafil are added surfactants, with Polysorbate 80 in the external phase and Labrafil in the inner phase.
reason: User-provided source text describes base nanoprecipitation in 1% PVA, NPR2 using 0.5% PVA + 0.5% Polysorbate 80 external phase, and NPR3 using 3.5 mg Labrafil in the inner phase.
impact: Layer3 GT and compare normalization should not treat PVA as the surfactant on the modified rows, and should preserve paper-local surfactant vs stabilizer roles.

title: 5GIF3D8W method/solvent split evidence from user paper check
decision: For 5GIF3D8W, PLGA rows use nanoprecipitation with acetone, while PCL rows use emulsion solvent evaporation with dichloromethane; the methods do not use a mixed solvent together.
reason: User-provided source text states nanoparticles were prepared by nanoprecipitation and emulsion solvent evaporation using PLGA polymers and PCL, respectively; acetone was used for PLGA and dichloromethane for PCL.
impact: Layer3 GT and compare normalization should split method_type and solvent_name by PLGA vs PCL row identity rather than carrying a combined shared value across both families.

title: YGA8VQKU shared-parameter evidence from user paper check
decision: For YGA8VQKU, acetone is explicitly reported as the solvent and Poloxamer 188 is explicitly reported as the aqueous-phase surfactant/stabilizer factor (cP188), but method_type is not explicitly classified into a governed canonical method label and LA/GA is not explicitly reported per formulation row.
reason: User-provided source text shows PLGA 75:25 polymers of two viscosities in materials, solvent displacement technique in methods, acetone as organic solvent, aqueous Poloxamer 188 solution as surfactant solution, and cP188 as a concentration factor in the experimental design.
impact: Layer3 GT/compare should treat acetone and Poloxamer 188 as explicit reported values, treat cP188 as a paper-local alias for Poloxamer 188, and avoid overstating method_type or row-level polymer_grade/LA:GA when the paper does not explicitly report them per formulation.

title: WIVUCMYG polymer-grade/shared-parameter evidence from user paper check
decision: For WIVUCMYG, Resomer® 753S is explicitly stated in the materials as the shared PLGA material across the paper, while the production method, solvent, and PVA shared parameters are also explicitly reported in the methods.
reason: User-provided source text states PLGA Resomer® 753S, PVA aqueous phase, acetone organic phase, and solvent displacement technique in the preparation section.
impact: Layer3 compare should treat the remaining mismatch class in WIVUCMYG primarily as a polymer-grade surface/carry-through issue rather than a shared-parameter binding ambiguity.

title: INMUTV7L shared-field and label semantics from user paper check
decision: For INMUTV7L, solvent displacement method and acetone are explicitly reported as shared preparation fields, while the formulation labels encode row-specific polymer/surfactant combinations such as '7 PLGA 10% PVA' meaning formulation 7 with PLGA-PEG 10% and PVA. The paper text also treats PVA, Tween 80®, and Lutrol F68 as surfactants.
reason: User-provided source text states solvent displacement method, acetone, 90 mg polymer in 5 mL acetone into 10 mL aqueous surfactant solution at pH 3.5, and explicitly explains the optimized concentrations of PVA, Tween 80®, and Lutrol F68 plus the formulation-label interpretation before Table 1.
impact: Layer3 GT/compare should use solvent displacement method as the explicit method_type surface, acetone as shared solvent, and row-label semantics to recover polymer/surfactant assignment on numbered formulations; the remaining no-unique-alignment row for '7 PLGA 10% PVA' is a compare/alignment issue rather than a paper-boundary ambiguity.

title: UFXX9WXE Layer3 source-text decode
decision: UFXX9WXE source text explicitly states PLGA 50:50 molecular weight 30,000–60,000; preparation method is emulsion solvent evaporation (nanoprecipitation) with acetone organic solvent and poloxamer 407 aqueous surfactant; Table 1 defines X1 polymer concentration 10/35/60 w/v, X2 surfactant concentration 2/8.50/15 w/v, X3 aqueous/organic phase ratio 2/6/10 v/v, X4 drug concentration 1/3/5 w/v; Table 2 decodes these as PLGA mg/mL, Poloxamer mg/mL, w/o phase volume ratio, and Drug conc. mg/mL; optimized formulation is X1 10 mg/mL, X2 9.42 mg/mL, X3 10, X4 4.5 mg/mL with experimental z-average 168.2 nm, PDI 0.08, entrapment 83.8%, and drug loading 8.7%.

title: Decision: Value authority and typed direct-value contract for Layer3 fields
decision: Row-local table-cell bindings are highest numeric value authority; row-local table assignments with typed headers are next; shared carrythrough must be source-backed and scoped; LLM semantics authorize scope but not numeric type; compare/source-CSV rebinding is diagnostic/fallback only. Direct benchmark values must not silently include derived %w/v×volume or ratio×mass calculations.
reason: Diagnostic 115 reduced risk rows 1812 to 1806, improved drug_mass_mg recall 0.395349 to 0.627907, improved polymer_mass_mg canonicalized accuracy 0.500000 to 0.685714, and removed polymer_mass_mg extra values 7 to 0 while lowering polymer_mass_mg recall due to rejecting invalid mass values.
impact: Use typed validators rather than value blocklists; mass fields reject identity, ratio, concentration, and volume tokens.

title: layer3_mass_carrythrough_derived
decision: Compare 123 added guarded shared preparation mass carrythrough and separate derived_mass_provenance_json. It improved polymer_mass_mg by 4 matches vs 115 (5GIF3D8W 50 mg rows), kept drug_mass_mg 115 gains, emitted zero derived rows, and left ACTIVE_RUN unchanged because diagnostic-only collateral remains.

title: Promote polymer_mass_mg scope-guard diagnostic baseline
decision: ACTIVE_RUN points to compare 130 as diagnostic baseline after scoped shared preparation polymer mass and ordinal_grid_semantics guard.
reason: General field contract reduced polymer_mass_mg pollution without paper-specific hard-fill; residual blocked_alignment/derived cases remain explicitly skipped.
impact: Layer3 error queue 1812->1799 vs compare 98; polymer_mass_mg extra 7->0 and canonical accuracy 0.5->1.0; recall lower because polluted nonempty values were removed.

title: S2-4a structural-only prompt freeze refresh
decision: S2-4a prompt rendering must reject stale cached table-summary text unless it already has summary_contract: structural_prompt_view_only and column_schema and has no table_role_hint, semantic_summary, or key_columns.
reason: Old frozen prompt artifacts can carry pre-LLM semantic labels in cached table summaries even after source-table rehydration is repaired; the generic guard prevents stale prompt surfaces from being re-promoted.
impact: Current refreshed artifact has 15/15 prompts, 92/92 structural table summaries, 0 forbidden semantic markers; diagnostic-only, not benchmark-valid final output.

title: p9 generic Stage2 authority reattachment and sweep guard repair
decision: Record p9 as a cross-paper generic Stage2 repair: clean-text authority reattachment for malformed table payloads remains deterministic S2-5 recovery, and S2-7 single-variable expansion must be source-observed endpoint/baseline gated; compare remains diagnostic-only.
reason: Lineage 429->434 from current working tree closes target L3H2RS2H/QLYKLPKT/5ZXYABSU/7ZS858NS and 5GIF3D8W, keeps QLYKLPKT 7/7 not over-counted, and has no count regression vs current 428 diagnosis baseline.
impact: Eligible only for diagnosis-baseline pointer consideration, not benchmark-valid promotion.

title: DeepSeek S2-4b initial trial configuration
decision: Use frozen S2-4a prompts; start S2-4b DeepSeek trial with deepseek-v4-flash, JSON Output enabled, thinking disabled, no cache settings; reserve pro/thinking-high for bounded comparisons.
reason: Flash likely sufficient for bounded semantic JSON task but needs validation; JSON Output improves structure; provider cache is automatic.
impact: DeepSeek provider/model parameters affect S2-4b raw-response lineage only and do not alter S2-4a or S2-5+.

title: Generic shared-parameter relation bundle
decision: Stage3 relation resolution may carry arbitrary source-backed shared field names; Stage5 preserves all resolved fields in shared_parameters_json and only projects governed core fields to typed benchmark columns.
reason: Per-field shared carrythrough rules do not scale to unknown paper-specific variables.
impact: Allows unbounded shared parameter transmission without widening the final table per variable; run145 -> run148 compare statuses unchanged.

title: Generic capability restoration for value materialization repairs
decision: Missing direct-value repairs must restore generic capability rather than patching specific papers or isolated fields; concrete papers are validation cases only.
reason: Paper-specific abbreviations, strings, and one-off field rules will not generalize across heterogeneous PLGA articles.
impact: Future S3/S5-2 repairs should be framed around paper-local material alias graphs, entity-value binding, scope-aware shared carrythrough, canonical promotion, and field-type validation with authority guards.

title: Subtype-scoped surfactant concentration carrythrough
decision: Rows with exactly one nanosphere/nanocapsule subtype may inherit a unique explicit preparation surfactant/stabilizer concentration from that subtype scope; duplicate unit extras are suppressed when GT value already contains the same unit.
reason: ACTIVE_RUN 135 had residual emulsifier_stabilizer_concentration_value missing cells with safe direct evidence in subtype-specific preparation paragraphs.
impact: Run 138 improved value recall 0.574324->0.682432 and overall error_rows 1773->1757 with no mismatch/extra/blocked increase.

title: polymer_mass scoped preparation carrythrough accepted
decision: Use source-backed direct preparation mass carrythrough for explicit parenthetical material labels and unique nanocarrier subtype scopes; exclude mg/mL concentration mentions from direct mass.
reason: Run 130 low recall reflected safe carrythrough gaps after pollution cleanup, not GT correction need.
impact: polymer_mass_mg recall improved 0.333333 to 0.612903 with accuracy 1.000000 and no match regressions.

title: Layer3 Stage3 shared numeric relation fields
decision: Stage3 resolved_relation_fields already emitted shared numeric/text fields, but Stage5 consumed only a narrow text whitelist. Expanded Stage5 resolved-field consumption with typed guards; removed centrifugation_g/time from core compare surface; run140 diagnostic error_rows 1757->1638 with mismatch/extra unchanged.

title: Layer3 concentration direct identity and surfactant binding split diagnostic
decision: Advance active diagnostic baseline to run142 after direct row-identity drug concentration recovery and bounded emulsifier/stabilizer name-concentration value/unit split; GT authority TSV was not mutated.
reason: Run142 produced a positive diagnostic delta vs run140: error_rows 1638->1598, present_and_match 1635->1675, missing 779->741, mismatch 85->83, extra/blocked unchanged.
impact: Improves direct concentration and surfactant/stabilizer compare accounting while preserving remaining QLYKLPKT value conflicts and WFDTQ4VX/YGA8VQKU residuals for source/GT adjudication.

title: Run143 O_volume_mL row-local direct volume authority activated
decision: Run143 activated row-local direct O_volume_mL authority diagnostically.

title: Shared entity-bound preparation parameter carrythrough
decision: Stage5 may carry a unique direct preparation-context solvent volume into organic_phase_volume_mL_* when the row already has the bound organic_solvent identity; globally shared drug_name can authorize direct drug mass carrythrough for eligible loaded rows.
reason: Run143 residual O_volume_mL errors were mostly final-table materialization gaps: rows had organic_solvent but no O_volume field bundle.
impact: Run145 vs run143: error_rows 1585->1515; O_volume_mL present_and_match 20->90 and missing 104->34; 70 missing_in_system->present_and_match; mismatch/extra/blocked unchanged.

title: Guarded emulsifier/material registry diagnostic baseline
decision: Accept run163 as the next diagnostic baseline after bounded row-local surfactant identity, guarded concentration materialization, source-backed material registry recovery, and compare-surface guards.
reason: run154 had positive gains but introduced polymer_grade extras, drug_name false-positive risk, and surfactant concentration mismatches; run163 repairs those regressions.
impact: Layer3 error_rows 1456->1417 vs run150; present_and_match 1817->1835; missing 599->583; present_but_mismatch 83->81; extra 186->165; blocked_alignment unchanged 588; benchmark_valid remains no.

decision: Generic endpoint-aware ratio direction repair accepted diagnostically: parse ratio endpoint variables/order for all ratio fields (LA:GA, polymer:drug, drug:polymer, polymer:solvent, phase water/oil or organic/aqueous); reverse only inverse-declared directions; avoid bare compact-ratio fallback for conflicting/direction-neutral fields. run166 error_rows 1607->1603 vs run165; benchmark_valid=no.

title: Generic concentration factor materialization
decision: Diagnostic run172 added bounded generic source-defined concentration-factor materialization for row-local X/cMaterial assignments; run166->run172 error_rows 1603->1559, missing 836->780; benchmark_valid=no.

title: Generic row-local formulation identity drug-name rebinding
decision: Accept bounded compare-surface extraction of drug_name from row-local formulation_identity_label/table_row_id when direct drug field is blank, rejecting blank/control and ambiguous labels.
reason: L3H2RS2H residual audit showed direct row labels carried XAN/3-MeOXAN while system drug_name surface was blank.
impact: 16 L3H2RS2H drug_name cells missing_in_system->present_and_match; error_rows 1559->1543; no mismatch/extra/blocked/non-target changes.

title: Stage2 table expansion preserves semantic scope role lookup through authority aliases
decision: Use semantic/original scope id fallback for table_variable_roles after authority payload reopen, and reject prose-spillover optimization sentences as row-level authority assignments.
reason: QLYKLPKT replay exposed that reopened table payload IDs can differ from the LLM semantic table scope, causing role lookup to miss; source-text spillover can also overcount an optimization sentence as a variable row.
impact: Bounded diagnostic replay restored QLYKLPKT final count to 7/7 while preserving LLM-first semantic authority and downstream Stage5 filtering of non-synthesis variants.

title: S2-1b high-confidence source denoise projection
decision: Add S2-1b between S2-1 scope resolution and S2-2 evidence construction; it emits denoised_for_stage2 text while preserving raw/current clean text as audit authority.
reason: DEV15 full-cleantext diagnostics showed obvious publisher/reference/header/footer noise entering candidate/evidence selection; existing cleanup was too late and too weak.
impact: Full original-source extraction should wait for no-live S2-1b->S2-2 acceptance; S2-1b must not perform formulation semantic discovery, table-importance selection, row-universe construction, or GT/source-anchor completion.

title: S2-4b explicit model boundary
decision: S2-4b live calls must receive an explicit --model; maintained code has no repository-wide default model identity.
reason: Model switching should affect only raw-response persistence at S2-4b, not S2-4a prompts, S2-5+ parsing/validation/projection, or unrelated live-call utilities.
impact: Changing --model creates a new S2-4b raw-response lineage; benchmark interpretation still requires aligned Stage2 completion lineage and GT authority.

title: S5-3 is residual source-evidenced gap filling, not database completion
decision: S5-3 must not assume every article reports every target field and must not target blank schema/final-table slots merely because they are empty.
reason: PLGA literature reporting is heterogeneous; absent values may be true non-reporting rather than extraction failure, and dense database-completion would create false positives.
impact: S5-3 scope must be gated by benchmark/GT missing_in_system evidence or audited source evidence; not_reported/unresolved/no-source blanks are not extraction targets; mismatches and blocked alignment need separate workflows.

title: Decision: Skip publisher chrome when compacting S2-2 table summary sample rows
decision: Compact TABLE_SUMMARY sample rows should continue scanning until non-noise source/table rows are found, rather than taking the first two data rows unconditionally.
reason: 5ZXYABSU and QLYKLPKT cleantext/table readthrough surfaces can place publisher chrome, title/author spillover, references, or submission footers at the top of extracted CSVs; using those as sample rows creates S2-2/S2-4a boundary noise while full payload authority remains valid.
impact: S2-2 DEV15 replay 438 and S2-4a/hard-gate replay 439 pass for 5ZXYABSU and QLYKLPKT. This is diagnostic-only and does not claim benchmark-valid improvement.
